title: Lathyrus atropatanus
intro: Lathyrus atropatanus, commonly known as the Iranian grass pea, is a type of legume that belongs to the Fabaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus atropatanus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lathyrus atropatanus include teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Lathyrus atropatanus is a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae. The plant is further classified under the tribe Loteae and the genus Lathyrus. The classification is based on its morphology and genetic characteristics, which are shared with other species within the same group. Further research is needed to determine the full classification of Lathyrus atropatanus.
uses: Lathyrus atropatanus, a species of vetch found in Iran, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's seeds have been studied for their antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The seeds may also have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Additionally, Lathyrus atropatanus is believed to have cardiovascular benefits, with some studies suggesting it may help lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure. More research is necessary to fully understand the medicinal uses of this plant and its potential applications in human health.

parts: Lathyrus atropatanus contains various medicinal parts, particularly its seeds, which are used to treat certain health conditions. The seeds are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful in treating fever and digestive issues. side_effects: Lathyrus atropatanus contains a toxin called ODAP or β-N-oxalylamino-L-alanine, which can cause neurological and neuromuscular symptoms in humans if consumed in large quantities. Prolonged consumption of Lathyrus atropatanus may lead to neurolathyrism, a condition characterized by muscle weakness, tremors, and paralysis. The toxin is particularly toxic to the nervous system and can cause long-term damage. If consumed during pregnancy, Lathyrus atropatanus can also cause birth defects and miscarriages. It is essential to exercise caution and consult a medical professional before consuming Lathyrus atropatanus or any other plant with similar toxins.
